judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with precise file/line citations; they are comparable, with Report 2 slightly more explicit on confirming migrations and Report 1 adding the shadow-sink code-path detail.

report_1.evidence: **`ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`** — `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2`. ... Change to `"false"`. ... **`PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`** — `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:4`. ... Remove it entirely (set to `""`). ... **`SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/"`** — `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:6`. ... Change to a production sink (e.g. `.../prod/observations/`) or set the sink to `""`

report_2.evidence: **`app.auto.tfvars:3` `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`** ... **Omit entirely.** ... **`app.auto.tfvars:4` `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`** ... **Omit entirely.** ... **`app.auto.tfvars:6` `SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = "s3://medislot-shadow/staging/observations/"`** ... This line must be **removed entirely**.

report_1.evidence: Because the build runs `--mode staging`, Vite loads `frontend/.env.staging`, which sets `VITE_API_BASE_URL=https://staging-api.example.com` ... That value is baked into the JS bundle. ... That one image is then deployed to **both** environments ... Consequence: the production frontend bundle ships hardcoded to call `https://staging-api.example.com`.

report_2.evidence: the **production bundle's API base URL comes from `frontend/.env.staging`** and points at `https://staging-api.example.com`. ... The same digest is deployed to both environments ... Fix: add `frontend/.env.production` with the real prod API URL, build a separate prod bundle with `--mode production`, deploy a distinct prod digest

report_1.evidence: **No database migration step.** The runbook (`docs/prod-runbook.md:6`) claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but `deploy.yml` contains no migration step at all. ... For a first prod launch against an empty database, nothing in the pipeline creates the schema.

report_2.evidence: `docs/prod-runbook.md:6` claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but `grep` for `alembic|migrat|upgrade head` finds **no migration step** in the workflow ... Per the first-apply trap, confirm explicitly whether migrations run on first prod deploy; as written, the schema would not be created/updated automatically.

report_1.evidence: **Production (broken as committed):** `infra/prod/main.tf:3` *also* sets `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"` — copied unchanged. But the prod job runs under `environment: production`, so its token sub is `...:environment:production`, which does **not** match ... It must be changed to `repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production`.

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` **still says `...environment:staging`** (copied verbatim ...). But the `deploy-prod` job runs with `environment: production` ... That does **not** match the staging condition ... Fix: prod must use `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"`
